question: For the online platform, which interpretation best reflects what can (and cannot) be inferred from the 100% stacked bar chart?

options.A: Q2 has the highest number of advertisements clicked compared to the other quarters.
options.B: Because each bar totals 100%, the absolute number of online searches is the same in every quarter.
options.C: The combined percentages of all three metrics indicate overall platform performance across the year.
options.D: We can compare the proportions of metrics within a quarter, but we cannot determine how their absolute quantities change across quarters.

explanation: 100% stacking normalizes unrelated categories to 100% within each quarter. Valid inferences include within-quarter ranking (e.g., one metric > another in every quarter). Invalid inferences include cross-quarter total comparisons or combining unrelated categories.
